 {"id":"bd-3didt","title":"Feature: Partial Synthesis Recovery","description":"## Summary
Enable recovery from partial ensemble failures by checkpointing mode outputs and allowing resume/rerun.

## Implementation
- Checkpoint after each completed mode (atomic write) with metadata: run_id, mode_id, context_hash, status.
- `ntm ensemble resume <run-id>` continues from last checkpoint.
- `ntm ensemble rerun-mode <run-id> <mode>` retries a specific failed mode.
- Cleanup checkpoints on successful completion; provide manual cleanup command.

## Logging
- Log checkpoint write/read events, resume actions, rerun targets.

## Tests
- Unit: checkpoint save/restore + integrity checks
- Integration: resume skips completed modes
- E2E: failure → resume → success flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checkpoints created after each mode
- [ ] Resume restores state correctly
- [ ] Rerun single mode works
- [ ] Cleanup occurs after success
- [ ] Logs show checkpoint lifecycle

## Dependencies
- EnsembleManager (bd-lp5h4)
- State persistence (bd-25wul)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:08:18.915853635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:07:49.783690679Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:07:49.783423907Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3didt","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3didt","depends_on_id":"bd-25wul","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3didt","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dqbk","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Restart Pane","description":"E2E tests for Robot Restart Pane with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Soft Exit Success
Spawn session with claude agent, verify running, call --robot-restart-pane, verify exit_method='soft', new agent running

### Scenario 2: Hard Kill Required
Spawn session, make agent unresponsive, call --robot-restart-pane, verify exit_method='kill', new agent running

### Scenario 3: With Bead Assignment
Spawn session, create test bead, call --robot-restart-pane --bead=X, verify bead_assigned, prompt in pane output

### Scenario 4: Invalid Pane
Call with non-existent pane, verify error response with available panes

### Scenario 5: Timing Verification
Instrument timing in restart sequence, verify 100ms gap between Ctrl-Cs, total time reasonable

## Test Infrastructure
ntm spawn test-restart --cc=1 --no-user

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 5 test scenarios implemented as bash scripts
2. Soft exit verified by checking agent gracefully terminates
3. Hard kill verified by checking agent forcibly terminated
4. Bead assignment verified by checking pane output contains prompt
5. Timing verification confirms 100ms gaps
6. Cleanup removes all test sessions
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. All test scenarios pass
2. Test logs follow JSON format specification
3. Cleanup removes all test sessions
4. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
5. Each scenario logs setup/execute/verify/result
6. Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure
7. Test runner script executable: scripts/e2e_robot_restart_pane.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:26:03.248632534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T04:34:13.533719738Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T04:34:13.533652603Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive E2E tests for Robot Restart Pane: TestE2ERobotRestartPane_SessionNotFound (error handling for nonexistent sessions), TestE2ERobotRestartPane_JSONStructure (required fields, arrays not null, timestamp format), TestE2ERobotRestartPane_DryRun (dry-run flag acceptance), TestE2ERobotRestartPane_ErrorResponse (error format, failed array reasons), TestE2ERobotRestartPane_OutputFormat (json format), TestE2ERobotRestartPane_BeadOption (invalid bead validation), TestE2ERobotRestartPane_SessionField (session echoed), TestE2ERobotRestartPane_VersionConsistency. Tests verify JSON response structure, error handling, and field consistency.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dqbk","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3dqbk","depends_on_id":"bd-ojx0p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-3dswi","title":"Task: Web UI sessions dashboard + pane viewer + command palette","description":"## Scope\nImplement Sessions deck: session list, session detail, pane stream viewer, and command palette.\n\n## Implementation\n- Sessions overview cards + filters.\n- Session detail page with agent counts and health.\n- Pane viewer with virtualization + auto‑scroll; supports prompt send.\n- Command palette (keyboard shortcut) wired to kernel metadata via REST.\n\n## Logging\n- Client logs for command execution + errors (dev only).\n\n## Tests\n- E2E: list sessions -> open -> send prompt -> see output.\n- Unit: virtualized pane component handles large outputs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Users can view pane output live and send prompts.\n- Command palette executes actions and reflects REST errors.\n","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:52:38.436898487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:32:54.077199086Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dswi","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3dswi","depends_on_id":"bd-35i7a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3dswi","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3dxk","title":"EPIC: End-of-Session Output Summarization","description":"**IDEA #13 - PROGRESS CLARITY (72% confidence)**

## What It Does
When ending a session, automatically generate a summary of what each agent accomplished, decisions made, and work remaining.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Users must mentally track what each agent did
- After: Clear record of session accomplishments
- Valuable for handoffs, standup reports, documentation

## Commands
ntm summarize myproject
ntm kill myproject --summarize

## Summary Generation Process
1. Capture full scrollback from all panes
2. Extract accomplishments using pattern matching
3. Extract key decisions
4. Integrate with git diff for concrete changes

## Technical Implementation
- GenerateSessionSummary() analyzing pane output
- extractAccomplishments() using pattern matching
- extractDecisions() identifying key choices
- Integration with git diff for concrete changes

## Dependencies
Depends on: Output Archive (for reliable capture)

## Success Criteria

1. ntm summarize generates coherent session summary
2. ntm kill --summarize works at session end
3. Accomplishments extracted from agent output
4. Decisions identified and logged
5. Git diffs included in summary
6. JSON output in robot mode
7. Unit tests for extraction logic (80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify summary generation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:27:30.387608415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:42.179031130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:42.178954267Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dxk","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":101,"issue_id":"bd-3dxk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added git diff/state integration to summaries by wiring ProjectDir+IncludeGitDiff in CLI summary and end-of-session monitor; added TestSummarizeSessionIncludesGitChanges. go test ./internal/summary passes.","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:10:58Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ef1l","title":"Task: WebSocket streaming tests","description":"## Scope\nAutomated tests for WebSocket streaming, resume, and backpressure.\n\n## Logging Requirements\n- Log subscription topics, seq numbers, and dropped counts.\n- Log reconnect attempts and cursor resets.\n\n## Tests\n- Subscribe to pane output and verify sequence continuity.\n- Disconnect/reconnect with since cursor.\n- Force backpressure and verify pane.output.dropped event.\n- Multi‑client fan‑out correctness.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- WS tests pass deterministically.\n- Resume and backpressure behaviors validated.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-opus-4.5","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:55:08.244291999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:23.246102626Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:23.245965070Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-cs5x4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-excs1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
